checks.finding: tasks.md T-001 through T-004 affect fail-closed review-state, migration-registry, or enforcement boundaries and consistently declare Risk: high; T-005 is reversible release metadata and declares Risk: medium. No sentinel-sensitive task is under-classified and no high-risk task is limited to copy, CSS, documentation, or tests.

tasks.md T-001 through T-004 each declare Required Workflow: tdd and include Done When items for saved Red→Green evidence and an independent review verdict. No Risk: critical task exists, so second-approver and signed-bundle requirements do not apply.

tasks.md T-004 Scope spans four distinct implementation areas—repository validation, CI, quality-gate, and downstream prechecks—and its Planned Files cross workflow configuration, skill policy, four runtime precheck adapters, and integration tests. This exceeds the check's three-area sizing indicator and makes one focused implementation session and isolated verification/rollback ambiguous; split T-004 into ordered enforcement-surface tasks or narrow its scope. This is Major because the oversized unit is likely to cause incomplete integration or verification.

acceptance-tests.md AC-002, AC-003, AC-004, AC-005, AC-006, AC-008, AC-009, AC-014 and tasks.md T-001/T-003/T-004 Done When sections plan invalid, malformed, traversal, stale, forged, unreadable, predecessor-inversion, CRLF/parity, and nonzero-failure paths; T-005 also requires a pre-bump version-consistency failure.
SKIP: tasks.md contains no standalone task declared as a unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance test task; functional tasks embed appropriately scoped verification instead.
tasks.md T-001 through T-004 each provide an explicit Rollback field and a Done When item requiring the rollback procedure to be documented or exercised against an isolated fixture.

tasks.md coordinates shared files through dependency ordering: T-004 follows T-003 and transitively T-001 before revisiting downstream prechecks, while T-005 follows T-004 before updating the shared validate-repository.ps1 release constant. Other primary implementation scopes are distinct.

tasks.md Blockers follow produced artifacts: T-003 consumes T-002's registry and the repaired review-contract behavior from T-001; T-004 integrates T-003's validator; T-005 follows the completed enforcement integration and updates a shared release-validation file. No missing or spurious dependency is evidenced.

tasks.md T-001 explicitly captures the Spec-Passed to Impl-precheck symptom, requires diagnosis of the canonical hash/path mismatch before implementation, records the original transition failure before the fix, and names regression/parity commands plus Red→Green evidence.
